departures.departure: Pre-staged raw files used instead of live web search.
departures.reason: Workshop reproducibility — the same output must render reliably before June 4, 2026. Live fetch produces non-deterministic content.

departures.departure: Directional EPS trajectory shown instead of old-vs-new model table.
departures.reason: No prior-quarter analyst model maintained for PANW in this pipeline. Consensus EPS history from yfinance used as substitute.

departures.departure: Peer NTM EV/Revenue multiples only — no DCF model.
departures.reason: No Bloomberg/FactSet access for live consensus forward estimates. Pre-staged peer files provide sufficient data for a directional price target range.

departures.departure: Structured JSON rendered as HTML (Tab 2) instead of 8–12 page DOCX.
departures.reason: Tab 2 of earnings_baseline.html is a web dashboard section. DOCX output format does not apply.

eps_nongaap.driver: Platformization velocity and operating leverage. Non-GAAP OI margin 30.3% — third consecutive quarter above 30%.

revenue.note: Revenue consensus not available in pre-staged files (null in yfinance free tier). Organic revenue growth +14.9% YoY. Chronosphere acquisition closed Feb 2026.

ngs_arr.note: Reported +33% YoY includes Chronosphere acquisition. Management cited +28% organic growth on the call. Platformized customer count: 1,550.

stock_reaction.driver: Market sold the Q3 guidance, not the Q2 beat. Q3 EPS guided $0.79 midpoint vs Q2 actual $1.03 — sequential step-down of $0.24. M&A integration costs (CyberArk, Chronosphere) diluting near-term EPS.

6_segment_geo.segment_note: Subscription/support is 80.2% of total revenue — high recurring base. Product revenue +22.1% YoY as platform consolidation drives firewall upgrades. FCF margin 14.8% is seasonally low (Q1 tends to be high due to annual billings timing). RPO $16.0B (+23.0% YoY) provides strong forward revenue visibility.
6_segment_geo.geo_note: Geographic breakdown not available in supplemental data. Full geo breakdown requires the 10-Q filing. Prior year Q2 FY25: Americas +13%, EMEA +18%, JPAC +17% YoY. Management cited 'broad-based strength across regions' on the Q2 FY26 call.

7_margin.driver_note: Non-GAAP gross margin 76.1% (-50bps YoY) — slight compression from mix shift toward lower-margin product revenue. Non-GAAP OI margin 30.3% (+190bps YoY) — third consecutive quarter above 30%, driven by operating leverage on subscription base. GAAP OI margin 15.3% (+460bps YoY improvement) — partly reflects Q2 FY25 litigation charge normalization. FCF margin 14.8% is seasonally low; Q1 FY26 was 68.2% due to annual billings concentration.

key_signals.q3_eps_step_down: Q3 midpoint $0.79 vs Q2 actual $1.03 — step-down of $0.24
key_signals.step_down_driver: M&A integration costs from CyberArk and Chronosphere acquisitions, both closed in Feb 2026. Near-term EPS dilution expected through H2 FY26.
key_signals.credibility_read: PANW has a consistent track record of conservative guidance and beating. The Q3 step-down has a clear M&A rationale. Risk: integration extends into FY27.
key_signals.ngs_arr_trajectory: Q3 NGS ARR guided $7.94–7.96B implies continued platform momentum.

9_estimate_revisions.note: No prior analyst model maintained. EPS trajectory shown instead of old-vs-new table.
9_estimate_revisions.direction: FY26 guidance raised. Near-term deceleration (Q3 $0.79) reflects M&A dilution. H2 FY26 implied $1.715 total (avg $0.858/quarter) suggests re-acceleration into Q4 FY26 beyond the initial integration dip.

10_valuation.note: No DCF. Peer NTM EV/Revenue multiples used for PT range.

ntm_build.assumption: FY27 H1 estimated at +15% YoY from FY26 H1 actuals

peer_table.note: Premium multiple reflects faster growth and ARR recovery
Margin leader; lower multiple reflects more hardware-oriented mix
EV not pre-staged; revenue miss this quarter notable

10_valuation.rationale: PANW trades at 11.7x TTM and 9.6x NTM EV/Revenue at the Feb 17 close of $163.5. Applying 10.0–12.0x NTM (discount to CRWD's 34.18x reflects PANW's lower growth rate of ~14.9% vs CRWD's ~23.0%) yields PT range $170–$203. Midpoint $186 implies +13.8% upside from post-earnings close.

11_rating.rationale: EPS beat of +9.9% and raised FY26 guidance (midpoint $3.675) support the thesis. Q3 EPS guidance ($0.79 midpoint) represents a $0.24 sequential step-down from Q2 actual — driven by M&A integration costs from CyberArk and Chronosphere, both acquired in Feb 2026. Stock fell 8.5% AH, partially absorbing the near-term headwind. At 9.6x NTM EV/Revenue, valuation reflects the integration discount. Platform thesis intact: NGS ARR +33.0% (organic +28%), 1,550 platformized customers, RPO $16.0B (+23.0% YoY). PT $186 based on 10.0–12.0x NTM EV/Revenue (13.8% upside). Q&A sentiment: 7 bullish / 0 bearish / 3 neutral exchanges.
key_risks: M&A integration (CyberArk, Chronosphere) extends beyond H2 FY26, further pressuring EPS
CRWD platform recovery intensifies — net new ARR +47% YoY signals competitive re-acceleration
Platformization strategy slows if customers consolidate more slowly than guided
NGS ARR organic growth gap vs. reported (28% vs 33%) widens if Chronosphere contribution disappoints
FCF margin recovery in H2 FY26 requires billings concentration — execution risk
